findings.evidence: `rg -n "show Module\.Finite" TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Zero.lean` reports line 132: `show Module.Finite R (zero R C) from by`.
findings.fix: Avoid the `show` by factoring a small lemma/instance for `Module.Finite R (ComoduleCat.zero R C)`, or add a comment explaining why the type cannot be reached by rewriting/inference directly.
findings.issue: `isFG_containsZero` uses an undocumented `show Module.Finite R (zero R C)` cast to bridge the bundled zero definition.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "show Module\.Finite" TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Zero.lean` reports line 148: `exact show Module.Finite R (ComoduleCat.of R C PUnit.{w + 1}) from inferInstance`.
findings.fix: Replace this with a reusable finite-zero lemma/instance or document why the explicit type bridge is necessary here.
findings.issue: `FGComoduleCat.zero` closes its property field with `exact show Module.Finite ... from inferInstance`, an undocumented `show` cast.

summary: The zero-object proofs are otherwise close to the Mathlib pattern, but the finite-generation witnesses use undocumented `show` casts, which this rubric treats as brittle proof structure.
